articles.title: Pregnant Calli Hauger Thackery finishes Boston Marathon through pain as US runner shares powerful moment
articles.description: Calli Hauger-Thackery and a US runner completed the Boston Marathon while pregnant, showing strength and determination. Hauger-Thackery faced pain but finished strong, while the US runner shared an emotional moment with her sister. Another runner, Jennifer Capone Haberek, ran after battling cancer. Together, their stories made this marathon about courage, not just competition.

articles.title: Lawrence Okolie breaks silence after failed drug test shocks boxing world, Tony Yoka fight now in serious doubt
articles.description: Lawrence Okolie has reacted after failing a pre-fight drug test ahead of his bout with Tony Yoka. He says the result may be linked to treatment for an elbow injury. The fight is now uncertain, with officials reviewing the case. Okolie has promised full cooperation and hopes the investigation will clear his name soon.

articles.title: Nicky Butt calls Alejandro Garnacho attitude disgrace as Chelsea struggles grow and Liam Rosenior responds to transfer rumours
articles.description: Nicky Butt has strongly criticised Alejandro Garnacho’s attitude during his Manchester United days, calling it a major issue. Meanwhile, Garnacho is struggling at Chelsea after a slow start. Despite transfer rumours, manager Liam Rosenior has backed the young winger. With Chelsea in poor form, all eyes are now on Garnacho to improve and deliver better performances.

The Coronet approach of Anaheim to protect McDavid gave other areas to act, displaying the depth of Edmonton and influencing the early momentum in this series of NHL playoffs.

articles.description: The Toronto Maple Leafs are pushing up their search for the due process of an NHL general manager, with Dallas Stars assistant general manager Scott White surfacing as one of the top candidates. The expedited schedule implies some kind of urgency with the front office vacancies across the league. Having several capable candidates in the running, Toronto seems to be on the verge of making an important leadership choice that can define its future in competitive terms in the short term.

Though these were heartbreaking losses, the Buffalo Bills remain focused on bouncing back on the field as they work on their roster.

articles.title: ‘Mental pressure blocking Olympic medals’: Rahul Banerjee on India’s hopes in archery
articles.description: Olympian Rahul Banerjee, a former Commonwealth Games gold medalist, has transitioned to coaching India's elite archers. He believes mental pressure is the final hurdle for Indian archery on the global stage. Banerjee is now focusing on mental training, incorporating meditation, visualization, and breathing techniques to refine his students' performance.

articles.title: WATCH: Cricketers In Bollywood! Virat, Rohit & Dhoni Reimagined In Fan-Made 3 Idiots Clip
articles.description:
The worlds of Bollywood and Cricket have collided yet again, but this time through a viral fan-made video that is breaking the internet. Using advanced AI face-swapping and clever editing, a creator has reimagined India’s biggest cricketing superstars as leads in some of Indian cinema’s most beloved blockbusters.

"3 Idiots" of Indian Cricket

The centerpiece of the viral clip features the legendary trio of MS Dhoni, Virat Kohli, and Rohit Sharma stepping into the shoes of Aamir Khan, Sharman Joshi, and R. Madhavan from the cult classic 3 Idiots. Fans have dubbed them the "real-life toppers" of the cricketing world, making the parody an instant hit.

Casting the "Cricketer-Actors":

The video doesn't stop there. It takes several other players and places them into perfectly suited cinematic roles:

Virat Kohli & Anushka Sharma in Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge: In a tribute to the "King of Romance," Virat replaces SRK in the iconic train scene, with Anushka reprising her real-life role as his leading lady.

WATCH VIDEO

😂 pic.twitter.com/CQHNjAhmH1

— Out Of Context Cricket (@GemsOfCricket) April 20, 2026

The Pathan Brothers in Brothers: Real-life siblings Irfan and Yusuf Pathan are reimagined in the high-stakes world of MMA from the movie Brothers, highlighting their famous camaraderie.

Yuzvendra Chahal in Kis Kisko Pyaar Karoon: Known for his off-field wit, Chahal fits perfectly into Kapil Sharma’s comedic role, playing a man juggling multiple chaotic relationships.

Arshdeep Singh in Bhaag Milkha Bhaag: The left-arm pacer’s lean frame and stamina are celebrated as he is cast as the legendary "Flying Sikh" in this intense sports biopic.

Social Media Frenzy

The clip has garnered millions of views within hours, with even some cricketers reportedly sharing the light-hearted edit on their Instagram stories. Fans are calling for a "Cricketer Cinematic Universe," noting that the casting for Arshdeep and the Pathan brothers is "scarily accurate."
